name: Chile Corn Cakes
recipeIngredient: 2 Cups fresh corn kernels, approximately 4 cobs
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan over medium-high heat, add olive oil. Sauté the peppers, corn, and onions for 23 minutes or until the onions begin to soften. Add the chili powder and cumin, and continue cooking for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
2. Deglaze the pan with chicken stock and continue cooking until most of the liquid has evaporated.
3. In a small bowl, sift together flour and baking powder. Add cornmeal, egg, milk, and butter, and mix well until very smooth. Stir in the corn mixture and cilantro, seasoning to taste.
4. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Drop corn batter into the skillet in large dollops and sauté for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden brown.
5. Remove with a spatula, place on paper towels, and pat dry. Cook in batches. Keep corn cakes warm in a 275°F oven until ready to serve.
Yield: 4 servings
